forecasts.reasoning: The resolution date for the question is July 1, 2025. The current date (cutoff) is April 11, 2026. Web searches reveal that the first rumors of Anthropic planning an IPO occurred in December 2025, when the Financial Times and Reuters reported they had hired a law firm to prepare for an IPO. Furthermore, an article from February 2026 specifically notes that Anthropic 'has not publicly endorsed plans to participate in an IPO', and April 2026 news consistently discusses the *impending* or *possible* news of an S-1 filing in the second half of 2026. Since no SEC Form S-1 was filed and no announcement was made on their official News page before July 1, 2025, this event did not occur. Following the rule to keep probabilities bounded between 0.05 and 0.95, I estimate 0.05.

After the resignation of Prime Minister Miloš Vučević on January 28, 2025, there were active demands for snap elections from protesters. However, President Aleksandar Vučić circumvented early elections by nominating Djuro Macut as Prime Minister on April 6, 2025, and forming a new cabinet which was approved by the parliament on April 16, 2025. During May and June 2025, Vučić and the ruling party flatly rejected protesters' demands for snap elections. An announcement to hold early elections in 2026 was later made by Vučić on December 28, 2025. Because this announcement occurred months after the July 1, 2025 resolution deadline, and there was no announcement of a specific election date prior to July 1, 2025, the resolution is clearly 'No'.

Multiple authoritative sources confirm that Starlink was licensed in Somalia in April 2025, but officially launched and went live on August 5, 2025. Articles from August repeatedly emphasize this as the official launch, detailing pricing and explicitly mentioning Elon Musk sharing the Starlink availability map marked as 'Available' across the country in August. Although a single Reuters headline from April 13 broadly claimed 'Musk says Starlink now available in Somalia', other contemporaneous sources clarify he had actually announced the granting of the license, not commercial availability. As the actual launch and mapping update to 'Available' occurred in August 2025, the website would not have shown Somalia as 'Available' before the July 1, 2025 deadline.

Based on historical reports from the 2026 forecast date, the US and Iran did not reach or announce a nuclear agreement prior to July 1, 2025. Instead, early June 2025 negotiations in Oman stalled when a 12-day war broke out, starting with Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities around June 12, 2025. The US eventually joined the strikes (Operation Midnight Hammer) in late June 2025. Negotiations completely broke down and did not resume until early 2026. Therefore, the chance that an agreement was signed or announced before July 1, 2025 is roughly zero.

Multiple independent sources, including reporting from tech news outlets (Business Insider Africa, Technext), Reddit users, and satellite map trackers (TS2), confirm that Starlink officially launched and updated its map to 'Available' in Chad on July 3, 2025. This date is strictly after the resolution deadline of before July 1, 2025. Therefore, the website did not show Chad as 'Available' before that deadline. I am setting the probability to the minimum compliant bound.

To resolve Yes, the r/Keep_Track "Project 2025 Tracker" must have marked the objective to "break up NOAA" as "complete" before July 1, 2025. Evidence clearly shows NOAA was not broken up by that date. Congress passed a full-year continuing resolution for NOAA in March 2025, preserving its funding. In July 2025, the Trump administration released a budget document proposing to cut NOAA's budget by 30% and its workforce by 18%, but this was merely a proposal pending congressional approval. By January 2026, Congress largely rejected these cuts and fully funded NOAA. Because the agency remained intact and structural changes were only at the proposal stage as of July 1, 2025, a rigorous tracker like Keep_Track would confidently maintain the status as 'in progress' or similar, not 'complete'. Therefore, resolution is almost certainly No.
